title: Neddoo
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Neddoo.png]] |name=Neddoo |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]][[Ewoks (1985) 7|''Ewoks'' (1985) 7]] |gender= |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair=*[[Color/Legends|Gray]] and blue *White {{C|[[Hair/Legends|beard]]}} |feathers= |eyes=Black |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Bright Tree Village tribe]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|How ''dare'' you pull a stunt like that?!|Neddoo, to Teebo|}} '''Neddoo''' was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] who lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Ewok [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], who was being affected by a [[loonee bird]], threw a bundle of reeds at Neddoo's hut as the former was attempting to repair it, destroying the structure. Neddoo was angered by the move and went to [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] to complain. ==Biography== {{Quote|Hi ya, Neddoo! Repairing your hut again? Ha ha!''"
"''Yes! I'm strengthening the support rods!|Teebo and Neddoo|}} Neddoo was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] member of the [[Bright Tree Village tribe|Ewok tribe]] in [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. On at least one occasion, Neddoo's hut had needed to be repaired. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], which include the events of [[Ewoks (1985) 7|''Ewoks'' 7]], to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Neddoo, who again needed to once again repair the hut, was approached by the [[Youngling/Legends|young]] Ewok [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], who was under the influence of a [[loonee bird]], which caused him to find everything around him hilarious and to play pranks. Neddoo was strengthening the home's support rods, explaining such to Teebo, and asked Teebo to help by passing a bundle of reeds. Instead of doing so, however, Teebo used a [[Wagon/Legends|wagon]] to launch the bundle at a startled Neddoo, which caused Neddoo to crash into the house, destroying it. [[File:NeddooTeebo.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Teebo laughs at Neddoo.]] Teebo laughed at Neddoo, who was shocked that the Ewok scout would do such a thing. It was then that Neddoo caught the effect of the loonee [[Bird/Legends|bird]], and also began laughing at the plight, with Teebo encouraging such mirth. However, once Teebo left, the effect wore off, and Neddoo questioned laughing about it. Neddoo later showed up at the [[Royal Hut|hut]] of [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] along with several other victims of Teebo's pranks in order to complain. By that [[Time/Legends|time]], Neddoo's [[Limb/Legends|arm]] was in a sling, while explaining to Chirpa what happened to the hut. Shortly after, the Ewok [[Shaman/Legends|medicine man]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] told everyone that Teebo was had been behaving strangely due to the loonee bird. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|He ''destroyed'' my ''hut!''|Neddoo, to Chirpa|}} Teebo considered Neddoo to be the "biggest sourpuss" in Bright Tree Village. Neddoo was angry at Teebo after the hut was destroyed, and questioned why he had pulled such a prank. After the effect of Teebo's laughter wore off, Neddoo could not understand what was funny. Neddoo then felt the need to complain about what Teebo had done to Chief Chirpa. Neddoo's [[Hair/Legends|fur]] was [[Color/Legends|gray]] and blue, and the Ewok had black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Neddoo also sported a white beard. ==Equipment== Neddoo wore green [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] with a hood. ==Behind the scenes== Neddoo appeared in the [[Ewoks (1985) 7|seventh issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], which was written by [[David Manak]], illustrated by [[Warren Kremer]], and published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[February 4]], [[1986]].[[Marvel Age 37|''Marvel Age'' 37]] ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Neddoo}} *[[Ewoks (1985) 7|''Ewoks'' (1985) 7]] {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Bright Tree tribe]] [[Category:Ewoks]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]]
